content_en:
Q. 217. What is the ruling on praying in a Masjid in which there is a grave?

A. Prayer in a Masjid in which there is a grave is of two types:

1. That the grave existed before the Masjid, so that the Masjid was built over the grave. Then it is obligatory to leave this Masjid and not to pray in it, and it is incumbent upon the person who

_ built it to destroy it. If he did not do so, it is incumbent upon the person in authority over the Muslims to destroy it.

2. That the Masjid existed before the grave, so that the deceased

"1 The two areas where waste exits.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 448 PHY! OST sabi

person is buried in it after the building of the Masjid. In this case, it is obligatory to open the grave, removing the deceased, and burying him with the people.

As for the Masjid, it is permissible to pray in it, with the condition that the grave is not in front of the worshippers, because the Prophet #% prohibited praying towards graves.

As for the grave of the Prophet #% which has been encompassed by the Prophet’s Masjid, it is well known that the Masjid of the Prophet # was built before he died, so it was not built over the grave. It is also well known that the Prophet #% was not buried in it; he was buried in his house which was separate from the Masjid.

Then during the rule of Al-Walid bin ‘Abdul-Malik, he wrote to his Governor in Al-Madinah, who was ‘Umar bin ‘Abdul-’Aziz, in the year 88 A. H. to demolish the Prophet’s Masjid and incorporate the rooms of the Prophet’s wives into it.

So ‘Umar gathered the prominent people and the scholars of Islamic Jurisprudence and read to them the letter from the Commander of the Faithful, Al-Walid and this was unacceptable to some of them and they said: Leaving it as it is, is more worthy of consideration. It is reported that Sa’id bin Al-Musayyib rejected the inclusion of ‘Aishah’s chamber, as if he feared that the grave would be taken as a Masjid. So, ‘Umar wrote this to AlWalid, and Al-Walid sent him a reply ordering him to implement it, leaving ‘Umar with no choice in the matter.

So, you see that the grave of the Prophet #% was not placed in the Masjid, nor was the Masjid built over it. Therefore there is no proof in it for one who claims that it is lawful to bury the dead in the Masjids or to build them over the graves. It has been confirmed from the Prophet # that he said:

‘Allah cursed the Jews and Christians who took the graves of their

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 449 Bel! os ld

Prophets as places of worship."

He said that when he was close to death, as a warning to the people against doing what those people did. When Umm Salamah, may Allah be pleased with her, mentioned a church that she had seen in the lands of Ethiopia, and the pictures it contained, the Prophet #% said:

tbl Be SEI GIGS Gs] Mans 05 “Those people, if a righteous man among them died or a righteous

worshipper, they would build a Masjid over his grave. Those people are the most evil of creation in before Allan.’'*!

It is reported on the authority of Ibn Mas‘ud, may Allah be pleased with him, that the Prophet #¢ said:

jelly eT hy BL EE te ON oo be Op

eles 5 fa Sgn “Verily, the most evil of people are those who will be overtaken by the Hour while they are alive and those who took the graves as places of worship.""*!

The believer does not accept the idea of following the way of the Jews and Christians, nor to be one of the most evil of people. Written on 7/4/1414 A. H.

") Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: The Hatefulness of Offering Prayers in Graveyards; and by Muslim in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Prohibition of Building Masjids Over Graves.

“1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Funerals, in the Chapter: Building a Masjid Over a Grave; and by Muslim in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Prohibition of Building Masjids Over Graves.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 450 PIE YI ost cyl

Q. 218. What is the ruling on prayer on the roof of the bathroom? What is the ruling of prayer on the roof of places where polluted wastes are collected?

A. There is no objection to prayer on the roofs of our well known bathrooms, because our bathrooms are not separate buildings, so their roof is that of the whole house. There is no objection to prayer on the roofs of places where polluted wastes are collected, according to the generality of the words of the Prophet #8:

“The earth has been made a place of prayer for me (and my followers) and a place to perform Tayammum.’'"!

Q. 219. What is the ruling on those who walk in their shoes on the floor of the Sacred Masjid?

A. Walking on the floor of the Sacred Masjid in shoes is unbefitting, because it will open up the door to the general masses, who do not esteem the Masjid, so they will come wearing shoes which are soiled with water, or they might even be soiled with impurities which they bring into the Sacred Masjid and thereby defile it.

Something which the Islamic Law encourages, but it is feared that it will entail harm, then it is an obligation to beware of this harm and abandon it. The established rule of the scholars is: If beneficial things and harmful things are in conflict and they are both equal, or the harmful things produce more damage than the good produced by the beneficial things, then preventing harm is more important than bringing about good. And thus Prophet (i.e. Muhammad #%) wanted to level the Ka‘bah and rebuild it on the foundations of Ibrahim, peace be upon him,

") Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Prayer.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 451 Ball is slit

but because the people had recently abandoned disbelief, he left this recommended deed, due to the fear that it would cause harm. He said to ‘Aishah, may Allah be pleased with her:

Si Lg Zk ye Aue sep Of Vg

5 ig Shs; seal sel

(abs Os py “If it were not that your people had recently abandoned disbelief, I would have levelled the Ka‘bah and rebuilt it upon the foundations of Ibrahim and made two doors for it: One door for the people to enter by and one door for them to leave by.”\"!

Q. 220. If it became clear to the worshipper that he deviated slightly from the Qiblah, should he repeat the prayer?

A. If he deviated only slightly, then it does not cause any harm. This is the case, except in the Sacred Masjid, because Qiblah for the worshipper in the Sacred Masjid is the Ka’bah itself. This is why the scholars have said that whoever is able to see the Ka’bah, it is an obligation upon him to face it directly. If it happened that a worshipper in the Sacred Masjid faced towards its general direction, but not towards the Ka’‘bah itself, then he must repeat his prayer, because his prayer is not valid; Allah, the Most High says:

“so turn your face in the direction of Al-Masjid Al-Haram (at Makkah). And wheresoever you people are, turn your faces (in

") Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Hajj, in the Chapter: The Virtue of Makkah and its Buildings; and by Muslim in the Book of Hajj, in the Chapter: The Levelling of the Ka’bah and its Rebuilding.

PRY OS I gS

prayer) in that direction.’""! However, if a person is far from the Ka‘bah and he is unable to see it, even though he may be in Makkah, then it is incumbent upon him to face its general direction, and if he errs a little, it does not harm. This is why the Prophet ## said to the people of Al-Madinah:

“what lies between the east and the west is a Qiblah.”'?}

This is because it is south of them, so everything between the east and the west is a Qrblah for them. Likewise, we say for example to those who pray towards the west that everything between the north and south is a Qiblah.

Q. 221. If a congregation prayed towards a direction other than the Qiblah, what is the ruling on that prayer?

A. This matter does not fall outside of two situations:

1. That they are in a place where it is not possible for them to ascertain the direction of the Qiblah, such as when they are on a journey and the sky is cloudy and they are unable to find guidance as to which direction the Qiblah lies in. In that case, if they prayed according to their best judgement, then it became clear to them that they were not facing towards the Qib/ah, there is nothing incumbent upon them. This is because they have kept their duty to Allah and feared Him as much as they are able and Allah, the Most High has said:

'] Reported by At-Tirmithi in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding the Fact That What Lies Between the East and the west is a Qiblah; and by Ibn Majah (1011); and by Al-Hakim in Al-Mustadrak (1:225), who said it was authentic, and Ath-Thahabi agreed with that.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 453 Ball sgh

“So keep your duty to Allah and 4 fear Him as much as you man can.

The Prophet #% said:

“If I command you to do something, then do as much as you are able,”"?)

And Allah, the Most High says regarding this matter:

Le i5 iD) Bl 255 3 3 i ct ils Sit 5) ‘And to Allah belong the east and the west, so wherever you turn (yourselves or your faces) there is the Face of Allah (and He is High above, over His Throne). Surely, Allah is All-Sufficient for His creatures’ needs, All-Knowing.’"?}

2. That they are in a place where it is possible for them to ascertain the direction of the Qiblah by asking, but they are neglectful and careless. In these circumstances, it is necessary for them to make up for the prayer they performed towards a direction other than that of the Qiblah, whether they were aware of their mistake before the time for the prayer passed or after it. They are guilty of error and wrongdoing in this situation: They erred regarding the matter of the Qiblah, because they did not deliberately deviate from it, but they are wrongdoers in that they were negligent and careless by not asking about it. Although we should know that deviating
content_id:
T. 217. Apa hukum salat di masjid yang di dalamnya terdapat kuburan?

J. Salat di masjid yang di dalamnya terdapat kuburan terbagi menjadi dua jenis:

1. Bahwa kuburan tersebut sudah ada sebelum masjid, sehingga masjid dibangun di atas kuburan. Maka wajib meninggalkan masjid ini dan tidak boleh salat di dalamnya, dan wajib bagi orang yang

membangunnya untuk menghancurkannya. Jika ia tidak melakukannya, maka wajib bagi penguasa kaum Muslimin untuk menghancurkannya.

2. Bahwa masjid sudah ada sebelum kuburan, sehingga orang yang

"1 Dua area tempat keluarnya kotoran.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 448 PHY! OST sabi

meninggal dikuburkan di dalamnya setelah pembangunan masjid. Dalam hal ini, wajib membuka kuburan, mengeluarkan jenazah, dan menguburkannya bersama kaum Muslimin.

Adapun masjidnya, boleh salat di dalamnya, dengan syarat kuburan tersebut tidak berada di depan orang yang salat, karena Nabi ﷺ melarang salat menghadap kuburan.

Adapun kuburan Nabi ﷺ yang telah tercakup dalam Masjid Nabawi, telah diketahui bahwa Masjid Nabawi ﷺ dibangun sebelum beliau wafat, sehingga masjid tersebut tidak dibangun di atas kuburan. Juga telah diketahui bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak dikuburkan di dalam masjid; beliau dikuburkan di rumahnya yang terpisah dari masjid.

Kemudian pada masa pemerintahan Al-Walid bin 'Abdul-Malik, beliau menulis surat kepada gubernurnya di Madinah, yaitu 'Umar bin 'Abdul-'Aziz, pada tahun 88 H. untuk meruntuhkan Masjid Nabawi dan memasukkan kamar-kamar istri Nabi ke dalamnya.

Maka 'Umar mengumpulkan para tokoh terkemuka dan ulama fiqh lalu membacakan kepada mereka surat dari Amirul Mukminin, Al-Walid. Hal ini tidak dapat diterima oleh sebagian mereka dan mereka berkata: Membiarkannya sebagaimana adanya lebih layak untuk dipertimbangkan. Diriwayatkan bahwa Sa'id bin Al-Musayyib menolak penyertaan kamar 'Aisyah, seolah-olah beliau khawatir kuburan tersebut akan dijadikan masjid. Maka 'Umar menuliskan hal ini kepada Al-Walid, dan Al-Walid mengirimkan balasan yang memerintahkannya untuk melaksanakannya, sehingga 'Umar tidak punya pilihan lain dalam hal ini.

Jadi, engkau dapat melihat bahwa kuburan Nabi ﷺ tidak ditempatkan di dalam masjid, dan masjid tidak dibangun di atas kuburannya. Oleh karena itu, tidak ada dalil di dalamnya bagi siapa pun yang mengklaim bahwa diperbolehkan mengubur mayat di dalam masjid atau membangun masjid di atas kuburan. Telah dikukuhkan dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda:

'Allah melaknat orang-orang Yahudi dan Nasrani yang menjadikan kuburan

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 449 Bel! os ld

para Nabi mereka sebagai tempat ibadah."

Beliau mengatakannya ketika mendekati ajalnya, sebagai peringatan kepada manusia agar tidak melakukan apa yang dilakukan kaum tersebut. Ketika Ummu Salamah radhiyallahu 'anha menyebutkan sebuah gereja yang pernah ia lihat di negeri Ethiopia dan gambar-gambar yang terdapat di dalamnya, Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

tbl Be SEI GIGS Gs] Mans 05 "Mereka itu, jika ada orang salih di antara mereka yang meninggal atau

seorang hamba yang salih, mereka membangun masjid di atas kuburannya. Mereka itulah seburuk-buruk makhluk di sisi Allah."(1)

Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Mas'ud radhiyallahu 'anhu bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

jelly eT hy BL EE te ON oo be Op

eles 5 fa Sgn "Sesungguhnya seburuk-buruk manusia adalah mereka yang akan dijumpai oleh Hari Kiamat sementara mereka masih hidup dan mereka yang menjadikan kuburan sebagai tempat ibadah."(2)

Seorang mukmin tidak menerima gagasan untuk mengikuti jalan orang-orang Yahudi dan Nasrani, dan tidak pula menjadi salah satu dari seburuk-buruk manusia. Ditulis pada 7/4/1414 H.

(1) Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Salat, pada Bab: Kebencian Melaksanakan Salat di Kuburan; dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid, pada Bab: Larangan Membangun Masjid di Atas Kuburan.

(2) Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Jenazah, pada Bab: Membangun Masjid di Atas Kuburan; dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid, pada Bab: Larangan Membangun Masjid di Atas Kuburan.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 450 PIE YI ost cyl

T. 218. Apa hukum salat di atas atap kamar mandi? Apa hukum salat di atas atap tempat pengumpulan limbah kotor?

J. Tidak ada keberatan untuk salat di atas atap kamar mandi kita yang sudah dikenal, karena kamar mandi kita bukan bangunan terpisah, sehingga atapnya adalah atap seluruh rumah. Tidak ada keberatan untuk salat di atas atap tempat pengumpulan limbah kotor, berdasarkan keumuman sabda Nabi ﷺ:

"Bumi telah dijadikan untukku (dan umatku) sebagai tempat salat dan tempat untuk bertayammum."(1)

T. 219. Apa hukum orang yang berjalan dengan memakai sepatu di atas lantai Masjidil Haram?

J. Berjalan di atas lantai Masjidil Haram dengan memakai sepatu tidaklah pantas, karena hal itu akan membuka pintu bagi masyarakat umum yang tidak menghormati masjid, sehingga mereka akan datang memakai sepatu yang terkena air, atau bahkan mungkin terkena najis yang mereka bawa masuk ke Masjidil Haram dan dengan demikian mengotorinya.

Sesuatu yang dianjurkan oleh syariat Islam, tetapi dikhawatirkan akan menimbulkan kerusakan, maka wajib berhati-hati terhadap kerusakan ini dan meninggalkannya. Kaidah yang ditetapkan oleh para ulama adalah: Jika hal-hal yang bermanfaat dan hal-hal yang berbahaya saling bertentangan dan keduanya sama, atau hal-hal yang berbahaya menghasilkan kerusakan lebih besar daripada kebaikan yang dihasilkan oleh hal-hal yang bermanfaat, maka mencegah kerusakan lebih diutamakan daripada mendatangkan kebaikan. Demikianlah Nabi (yaitu Muhammad ﷺ) ingin meratakan Ka'bah dan membangunnya kembali di atas pondasi Ibrahim 'alaihissalam,

(1) Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Salat.

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 451 Ball is slit

tetapi karena kaum tersebut baru saja meninggalkan kekufuran, beliau meninggalkan amalan yang dianjurkan ini, karena khawatir akan menimbulkan kerusakan. Beliau bersabda kepada 'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha:

Si Lg Zk ye Aue sep Of Vg

5 ig Shs; seal sel

(abs Os py "Seandainya kaummu tidak baru saja meninggalkan kekufuran, niscaya aku akan meratakan Ka'bah dan membangunnya kembali di atas pondasi Ibrahim dan membuat dua pintu untuknya: Satu pintu untuk orang-orang masuk dan satu pintu untuk mereka keluar."(1)

T. 220. Jika jelas bagi orang yang salat bahwa ia sedikit menyimpang dari kiblat, apakah ia harus mengulangi salatnya?

J. Jika ia hanya sedikit menyimpang, maka hal itu tidak menimbulkan bahaya. Ini berlaku, kecuali di Masjidil Haram, karena kiblat bagi orang yang salat di Masjidil Haram adalah Ka'bah itu sendiri. Inilah sebabnya para ulama mengatakan bahwa barangsiapa yang mampu melihat Ka'bah, maka wajib baginya menghadapnya secara langsung. Jika terjadi seorang yang salat di Masjidil Haram menghadap ke arah umumnya, tetapi tidak ke Ka'bah itu sendiri, maka ia harus mengulangi salatnya, karena salatnya tidak sah; Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

"maka palingkanlah wajahmu ke arah Masjidil Haram (di Makkah). Dan di mana saja kamu berada, palingkanlah wajahmu (dalam

(1) Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Haji, pada Bab: Keutamaan Makkah dan Bangunan-bangunannya; dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Haji, pada Bab: Meratakan Ka'bah dan Membangunnya Kembali.

PRY OS I gS

salat) ke arahnya."(1) Namun, jika seseorang jauh dari Ka'bah dan tidak mampu melihatnya, meskipun ia mungkin berada di Makkah, maka wajib baginya menghadap ke arah umumnya, dan jika ia sedikit keliru, hal itu tidak membahayakan. Inilah sebabnya Nabi ﷺ bersabda kepada penduduk Madinah:

"Apa yang berada di antara timur dan barat adalah kiblat."(2)

Ini karena kiblat berada di selatan mereka, sehingga segala sesuatu antara timur dan barat adalah kiblat bagi mereka. Demikian pula, kita katakan misalnya kepada mereka yang salat menghadap barat bahwa segala sesuatu antara utara dan selatan adalah kiblat.

T. 221. Jika suatu jamaah salat menghadap ke arah selain kiblat, apa hukum salat tersebut?

J. Masalah ini tidak lepas dari dua keadaan:

1. Bahwa mereka berada di tempat yang tidak memungkinkan mereka untuk memastikan arah kiblat, seperti ketika mereka dalam perjalanan dan langit berawan dan mereka tidak mampu menemukan petunjuk ke arah mana kiblat berada. Dalam hal ini, jika mereka salat berdasarkan penilaian terbaik mereka, kemudian menjadi jelas bagi mereka bahwa mereka tidak menghadap kiblat, tidak ada kewajiban apa pun atas mereka. Ini karena mereka telah menunaikan kewajiban mereka kepada Allah dan bertakwa kepada-Nya semampu mereka dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

'] Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi dalam Kitab Salah, dalam Bab: Apa yang Diriwayatkan Mengenai Bahwa Apa yang Terletak Antara Timur dan Barat adalah Kiblat; dan oleh Ibn Majah (1011); dan oleh Al-Hakim dalam Al-Mustadrak (1:225), yang menyatakannya sahih, dan Adz-Dzahabi menyetujuinya.

Fatwa-Fatwa Berkaitan dengan Salah 453 Ball sgh

"Maka bertakwalah kepada Allah dan takutlah kepada-Nya sebisa kalian.

Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Jika aku memerintahkan kalian untuk melakukan sesuatu, maka lakukanlah semampu kalian."

Dan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman mengenai hal ini:

Le i5 iD) Bl 255 3 3 i ct ils Sit 5) 'Dan milik Allah-lah timur dan barat, maka ke mana pun kamu menghadap di situlah Wajah Allah (dan Dia Maha Tinggi, di atas Arsy-Nya). Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Luas (rahmat-Nya) lagi Maha Mengetahui.'"

2. Bahwa mereka berada di suatu tempat di mana memungkinkan bagi mereka untuk memastikan arah kiblat dengan bertanya, tetapi mereka lalai dan ceroboh. Dalam keadaan seperti ini, mereka wajib mengulang salah yang mereka laksanakan ke arah selain kiblat, baik mereka menyadari kesalahan mereka sebelum waktu salah habis maupun sesudahnya. Mereka bersalah karena kelalaian dan kesalahan dalam situasi ini: Mereka keliru mengenai masalah kiblat, karena mereka tidak sengaja menyimpang darinya, tetapi mereka berdosa karena lalai dan ceroboh dengan tidak bertanya tentangnya. Meskipun kita harus mengetahui bahwa menyimpang
content_ar: Q. 217. What is the ruling on praying in a المسجد in which there is a grave? A. الصلاة in a المسجد in which there is a grave is of two types: 1. That the grave existed before the المسجد, so that the المسجد was built over the grave. Then it is واجب to leave this المسجد and not to pray in it, and it is incumbent upon the person who _ built it to destroy it. If he did not do so, it is incumbent upon the person in authority over the المسلمون to destroy it. 2. That the المسجد existed before the grave, so that the deceased "1 The two areas where waste exits. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 448 PHY! OST sabi person is buried in it after the building of the المسجد. In this case, it is واجب to open the grave, removing the deceased, and burying him with the people. As for the المسجد, it is مباح to pray in it, with the condition that the grave is not in front of the worshippers, because النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% prohibited praying towards graves. As for the grave of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% which has been encompassed by the Prophet’s المسجد, it is well known that the المسجد of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # was built before he died, so it was not built over the grave. It is also well known that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% was not buried in it; he was buried in his house which was separate from the المسجد. Then during the rule of Al-Walid bin ‘Abdul-Malik, he wrote to his Governor in Al-المدينة المنورة, who was ‘Umar bin ‘Abdul-’Aziz, in the year 88 A. H. to demolish the Prophet’s المسجد and incorporate the rooms of the Prophet’s wives into it. So ‘Umar gathered the prominent people and the العلماء of الإسلامي الفقه الإسلامي and read to them the letter from the Commander of the Faithful, Al-Walid and this was unacceptable to some of them and they said: Leaving it as it is, is more worthy of consideration. It is reported that Sa’id bin Al-Musayyib rejected the inclusion of ‘Aishah’s chamber, as if he feared that the grave would be taken as a المسجد. So, ‘Umar wrote this to AlWalid, and Al-Walid sent him a reply ordering him to implement it, leaving ‘Umar with no choice in the matter. So, you see that the grave of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% was not placed in the المسجد, nor was the المسجد built over it. Therefore there is no proof in it for one who claims that it is lawful to bury the dead in the Masjids or to build them over the graves. It has been confirmed from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # that he said: ‘الله cursed the Jews and Christians who took the graves of their الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 449 Bel! os ld Prophets as places of worship." He said that when he was close to death, as a warning to the people against doing what those people did. When Umm Salamah, رضي الله عنها, mentioned a church that she had seen in the lands of Ethiopia, and the pictures it contained, النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% said: tbl Be SEI GIGS Gs] Mans 05 “Those people, if a righteous man among them died or a righteous worshipper, they would build a المسجد over his grave. Those people are the most evil of creation in before Allan.’'*! It is reported on the authority of Ibn Mas‘ud, رضي الله عنه, that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #¢ said: jelly eT hy BL EE te ON oo be Op eles 5 fa Sgn “Verily, the most evil of people are those who will be overtaken by the Hour while they are alive and those who took the graves as places of worship.""*! The believer does not accept the idea of following the way of the Jews and Christians, nor to be one of the most evil of people. Written on 7/4/1414 A. H. ") Reported by البخاري in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: The Hatefulness of Offering Prayers in Graveyards; and by المسلم in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Prohibition of Building Masjids Over Graves. “1 Reported by البخاري in the Book of Funerals, in the Chapter: Building a المسجد Over a Grave; and by المسلم in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Prohibition of Building Masjids Over Graves. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 450 PIE YI ost cyl Q. 218. What is the ruling on الصلاة on the roof of the bathroom? What is the ruling of الصلاة on the roof of places where polluted wastes are collected? A. There is no objection to الصلاة on the roofs of our well known bathrooms, because our bathrooms are not separate buildings, so their roof is that of the whole house. There is no objection to الصلاة on the roofs of places where polluted wastes are collected, according to the generality of the words of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #8: “The earth has been made a place of الصلاة for me (and my followers) and a place to perform التيمم.’'"! Q. 219. What is the ruling on those who walk in their shoes on the floor of the Sacred المسجد? A. Walking on the floor of the Sacred المسجد in shoes is unbefitting, because it will open up the door to the general masses, who do not esteem the المسجد, so they will come wearing shoes which are soiled with water, or they might even be soiled with impurities which they bring into the Sacred المسجد and thereby defile it. Something which the الإسلامي Law encourages, but it is feared that it will entail harm, then it is an obligation to beware of this harm and abandon it. The established rule of the العلماء is: If beneficial things and harmful things are in conflict and they are both equal, or the harmful things produce more damage than the good produced by the beneficial things, then preventing harm is more important than bringing about good. And thus Prophet (i.e. Muhammad #%) wanted to level the Ka‘bah and rebuild it on the foundations of Ibrahim, peace be upon him, ") Reported by البخاري in the Book of الصلاة. الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 451 Ball is slit but because the people had recently abandoned disbelief, he left this مستحب deed, due to the fear that it would cause harm. He said to ‘Aishah, رضي الله عنها: Si Lg Zk ye Aue sep Of Vg 5 ig Shs; seal sel (abs Os py “If it were not that your people had recently abandoned disbelief, I would have levelled the Ka‘bah and rebuilt it upon the foundations of Ibrahim and made two doors for it: One door for the people to enter by and one door for them to leave by.”\"! Q. 220. If it became clear to the worshipper that he deviated slightly from the القبلة, should he repeat the الصلاة? A. If he deviated only slightly, then it does not cause any harm. This is the case, except in the Sacred المسجد, because القبلة for the worshipper in the Sacred المسجد is the Ka’bah itself. This is why the العلماء have said that whoever is able to see the Ka’bah, it is an obligation upon him to face it directly. If it happened that a worshipper in the Sacred المسجد faced towards its general direction, but not towards the Ka’‘bah itself, then he must repeat his الصلاة, because his الصلاة is not valid; الله, the Most High says: “so turn your face in the direction of Al-المسجد Al-الحرام (at مكة المكرمة). And wheresoever you people are, turn your faces (in ") Reported by البخاري in the Book of الحج, in the Chapter: The الفضيلة of مكة المكرمة and its Buildings; and by المسلم in the Book of الحج, in the Chapter: The Levelling of the Ka’bah and its Rebuilding. PRY OS I gS الصلاة) in that direction.’""! However, if a person is far from the Ka‘bah and he is unable to see it, even though he may be in مكة المكرمة, then it is incumbent upon him to face its general direction, and if he errs a little, it does not harm. This is why النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم ## said to the people of Al-المدينة المنورة: “what lies between the east and the west is a القبلة.”'?} This is because it is south of them, so everything between the east and the west is a Qrblah for them. Likewise, we say for example to those who pray towards the west that everything between the north and south is a القبلة. Q. 221. If a congregation prayed towards a direction other than the القبلة, what is the ruling on that الصلاة? A. This matter does not fall outside of two situations: 1. That they are in a place where it is not possible for them to ascertain the direction of the القبلة, such as when they are on a journey and the sky is cloudy and they are unable to find guidance as to which direction the القبلة lies in. In that case, if they prayed according to their best judgement, then it became clear to them that they were not facing towards the Qib/ah, there is nothing incumbent upon them. This is because they have kept their duty to الله and feared Him as much as they are able and الله, the Most High has said: '] Reported by At-Tirmithi in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: What Has Been Said Regarding the Fact That What Lies Between the East and the west is a القبلة; and by ابن ماجه (1011); and by Al-Hakim in Al-Mustadrak (1:225), who said it was صحيح, and Ath-Thahabi agreed with that. الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 453 Ball sgh “So keep your duty to الله and 4 fear Him as much as you man can. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% said: “If I command you to do something, then do as much as you are able,”"?) And الله, the Most High says regarding this matter: Le i5 iD) Bl 255 3 3 i ct ils Sit 5) ‘And to الله belong the east and the west, so wherever you turn (yourselves or your faces) there is the Face of الله (and He is High above, over His Throne). Surely, الله is All-Sufficient for His creatures’ needs, All-Knowing.’"?} 2. That they are in a place where it is possible for them to ascertain the direction of the القبلة by asking, but they are neglectful and careless. In these circumstances, it is necessary for them to make up for the الصلاة they performed towards a direction other than that of the القبلة, whether they were aware of their mistake before the time for the الصلاة passed or after it. They are guilty of error and wrongdoing in this situation: They erred regarding the matter of the القبلة, because they did not deliberately deviate from it, but they are wrongdoers in that they were negligent and careless by not asking about it.
